req.title: Vital signs validation
req.statement: An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range; out-of-range shall be flagged for review.
expected_outcomes: flag raised if out of range

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-019 requires that vital-signs Observations are validated against clinically plausible ranges, stored on success, and flagged for review when out-of-range. I selected: (1) EP positive — a normal heart-rate value from the valid equivalence class, confirming 201 and no flag; (2) NEG negative — a physiologically impossible heart-rate (300 bpm) that must trigger a review task; (3) BVA boundary — heart-rate exactly at the accepted upper boundary (200 bpm) to probe fence-post behavior. A fourth DT case covers a multi-vital payload where one value is in-range and one is out-of-range, exercising the decision logic per-component. Each test uses a clinician-role auth fixture, posts to /Observation, and asserts both HTTP status and the flag/review-task state.

tests.name: test_vital_signs_normal_heart_rate_stored_no_flag
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
No pre-existing Observation with the test identifier exists
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No extension or tag indicating out-of-range flag
No review Task created for this Observation
trace: R-HLT-019: value in clinically plausible range -> observation stored
R-HLT-019: out-of-range flag NOT raised for normal value
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_normal_heart_rate_stored_no_flag(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": unique_id,
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "/min", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Observation"
    tags = [t.get("code") for t in body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
    assert "out-of-range" not in tags, "Unexpected out-of-range flag on normal value"
    task_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Task", params={"focus": f"Observation/{unique_id}"}, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    assert task_resp.json().get("total", 0) == 0, "Review Task must not exist for in-range observation"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_impossible_heart_rate_flagged_and_review_task_created
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
No pre-existing Observation with the test identifier exists
actions: POST /Observation with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity.value=300 (physiologically impossible)
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query /Task for a review task referencing the created Observation
expected: HTTP 201 Created (observation accepted but flagged) OR HTTP 422 with flag details
Response body or subsequent GET contains out-of-range tag/extension
A review Task resource exists referencing the Observation
trace: R-HLT-019: out-of-range value -> flag raised for review
R-HLT-019: error pathway -> review task created
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_impossible_heart_rate_flagged_and_review_task_created(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": unique_id,
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 300, "unit": "/min", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 422), f"Unexpected status {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    if resp.status_code == 201:
        body = resp.json()
        tags = [t.get("code") for t in body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
        assert "out-of-range" in tags, "Missing out-of-range flag on impossible value"
        task_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Task", params={"focus": f"Observation/{unique_id}"}, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
        assert task_resp.status_code == 200
        assert task_resp.json().get("total", 0) >= 1, "Review Task must be created for out-of-range observation"
    else:
        body = resp.json()
        issues = [i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
        assert any("range" in d.lower() or "flag" in d.lower() for d in issues), f"Expected range/flag diagnostic, got: {issues}"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_heart_rate_at_upper_boundary_behavior
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
System upper boundary for heart-rate is defined as 200 bpm
No pre-existing Observation with the test identifier exists
actions: POST /Observation with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity.value=200 (exact upper boundary)
Capture HTTP response status, body, and any flag tags
Query /Task to check for review task
expected: If boundary is inclusive: no out-of-range flag; no review Task
If boundary is exclusive: out-of-range flag present; review Task created
Behavior is consistent and deterministic for value=200
trace: R-HLT-019: boundary value at upper limit of plausible range -> deterministic flag/no-flag outcome
R-HLT-019: BVA fence-post probe for clinically plausible range definition
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

UPPER_BOUNDARY = 200

def test_vital_signs_heart_rate_at_upper_boundary_behavior(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id, vital_signs_range_config):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": unique_id,
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": UPPER_BOUNDARY, "unit": "/min", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    tags = [t.get("code") for t in body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
    upper_inclusive = vital_signs_range_config.get("heart_rate_upper_inclusive", True)
    task_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Task", params={"focus": f"Observation/{unique_id}"}, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    task_count = task_resp.json().get("total", 0)
    if upper_inclusive:
        assert "out-of-range" not in tags, "Boundary (inclusive) must not be flagged"
        assert task_count == 0, "No review Task expected for inclusive boundary value"
    else:
        assert "out-of-range" in tags, "Boundary (exclusive) must be flagged"
        assert task_count >= 1, "Review Task expected for exclusive boundary value"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_mixed_observation_partial_flag_decision
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
System supports multi-component Observation for vital-signs
No pre-existing Observation with the test identifier exists
actions: POST /Observation with category=vital-signs containing two components: systolic BP=120 (in-range) and diastolic BP=200 (out-of-range)
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query /Task for review task referencing the Observation
expected: Observation stored with out-of-range flag (due to diastolic component)
Review Task created referencing the Observation
In-range component (systolic) does not independently suppress the flag
trace: R-HLT-019: decision table — one component in-range, one out-of-range -> flag raised
R-HLT-019: partial-validity decision row -> review task created
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_mixed_observation_partial_flag_decision(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": unique_id,
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "55284-4", "display": "Blood pressure"}]},
        "component": [
            {"code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8480-6", "display": "Systolic BP"}]}, "valueQuantity": {"value": 120, "unit": "mmHg", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "mm[Hg]"}},
            {"code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8462-4", "display": "Diastolic BP"}]}, "valueQuantity": {"value": 200, "unit": "mmHg", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "mm[Hg]"}}
        ]
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    tags = [t.get("code") for t in body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
    assert "out-of-range" in tags, "Expected out-of-range flag due to abnormal diastolic component"
    task_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Task", params={"focus": f"Observation/{unique_id}"}, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    assert task_resp.json().get("total", 0) >= 1, "Review Task must exist when any component is out-of-range"
